description: To 'hewe' is to move a hand, cloth, or similar surface across something in order to remove or spread material. A person can wipe a wet table or tears from a face. 'sawa' (wash) uses liquid cleaning, while 'rohi' (rub) keeps surfaces sliding together under pressure.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced air pass through the open glottis for 'h' and move into stressed middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then release the rounding into middle-height front 'e'.
sound_symbolism: The word has no complete oral closure: open breath moves through a rounded glide and returns to the same front vowel. Its light, uninterrupted contour feels much like one pass of a cloth across a surface.
usage_notes: The surface or body part being wiped precedes 'hewe'. The wiping tool may take 'roe' (INS), and a nearby phrase can name what is removed or spread. In noun position, 'hewe' is a wipe or wiping action.

semantic_domains.activity: a clearing or spreading stroke made with a hand, cloth, or similar surface
semantic_domains.physical: material removed from or spread across a surface through sliding contact
